clinical_history: This ECG from a 74 year old man shows all of the following EXCEPT for which ONE of the following findings?
diagnosis: The ECG shows marked sinus bradycardia at a rate of about 36 /min. Waveform findings are also consistent with left atrial abnormality (broad notched P waves, e.g. in V4; biphasic in V1 with broad negative component on magnified view); left ventricular hypertrophy (R wave of 13 mm in aVL) and borderline left axis deviation (about -20 degrees).The QRS duration is borderline prolonged (computerized measurement is 116 ms), but complete left bundle branch block is not present. The patient required a dual-chamber pacemaker for symptomatic sinus syndrome, with an underlying history of obstructive sleep apnea.
